chunk_summary: Persistent puree dependence at ten months is a feeding-skill issue requiring texture progression coaching and responsive feeding to prevent dietary diversity and mealtime conflicts.
learning_objectives: Understand the clinical implications of persistent puree dependence beyond 9–10 months.
Learn staged texture advancement strategies for infants with feeding-skill delays.
Identify referral triggers for aspiration risks or failure to progress textures despite coaching.
key_facts: Prolonged puree-only feeding beyond 9–10 months increases risk of texture refusal and reduced dietary diversity.
Meals should be limited to ≤30 minutes with the child seated upright to avoid distractions and coercion.
Staged texture ladder: Week 1 fork-mashed foods, Week 2 minced/chopped foods, Week 3–4 family foods.
High milk intake and lack of finger foods often result in low Minimum Dietary Diversity (MDD).
Referral triggers include aspiration red flags or failure to advance textures within 2–4 weeks.
reference_quote: Prolonged puree-only feeding beyond about 9–10 months increases risk of entrenched texture refusal, reduced dietary diversity, and caregiver–child mealtime conflict. (WHO Guiding Principles for Complementary Feeding)
document_key_topics: Texture progression as a feeding milestone
